headlines.title: OPEC’s Oil Demand Bet Is on India, Not Europe
headlines.summary: Every year, OPEC releases a long-term outlook that says oil demand will keep growing. Every year, critics roll their eyes. This year, OPEC doubled down. In its World Oil Outlook 2026, the producer group said global oil demand will climb from 105.1 million barrels per day in 2025

headlines.title: Oil Prices Are Crashing. Inventories Tell A Different Story.
headlines.summary: Oil traders have gone from pricing in the worst supply disruption in modern history to pricing in a recovery that hasn't happened yet. Inventory data suggests they’re getting ahead of themselves. Brent crude fell below $77 per barrel on Thursday, the lowest level since the early

headlines.title: High Tanker Rates Disrupt Persian Gulf Oil Shipments to Asia

headlines.title: India's Pump Prices Stuck Even As Global Crude Crashes
headlines.summary: India’s fuel prices will remain elevated for some time despite the declining international crude prices, according to India’s Union Minister of State for Petroleum and Natural Gas, Suresh Gopi. headlines.title: JPMorgan: European Stocks Are Attractively Cheap After Oil Price Slump
headlines.summary: Europe’s relatively cheap stocks could become attractive for investors as the oil prices plunged and markets hope the Hormuz crisis is over, according to JPMorgan. headlines.title: Argus: U.S-Iran Deal Won’t Lead to One-Way Traffic to Plunging Oil Prices
headlines.summary: The uncertain pace of supply recovery in the Middle East and the continued rapid drawdowns of global inventories make the case that oil price volatility would drag on during the 60-day U.S.-Iran negotiation window, David Fyfe, Chief Economist at Argus Media, told CNBC on Thursday

headlines.title: India’s Energy Import Bill Jumps 82% Due to High Oil Prices
headlines.summary: India’s imports of crude and LNG jumped in May from April, as arrivals of non-Middle Eastern cargoes accelerated, and the country's energy import bill soared by nearly 82% from a year earlier amid higher energy prices.
